title: MEDIUM: HTTP Signature verification does not require host header
description:
verify_and_fetch_actor() in inbox.rs requires (request-target), digest, and date in the signed headers, but does NOT require the host header.

Since (request-target) only contains the path (e.g., 'post /inbox'), a signed request to https://victim.example/inbox can be replayed to https://attacker.example/inbox within the 12-hour Date freshness window. The attacker would receive the activity body and potentially learn about private follows, DMs, or other non-public activities.

Practical impact is limited because:
1. The attacker needs to intercept the original request (network-level attacker)
2. The path must match on both servers
3. The 12-hour window limits replay duration

But this is the standard ActivityPub signature hardening that Mastodon, Pleroma, and other implementations enforce.

Fix: add host to the required signed headers:
```rust
if !sig_params.headers_list.iter().any(|h| h == "host") {
    return Err(AppError::unauthorized("Signature must include host header"));
}
```

File: src/inbox.rs lines 547-565
close_reason: Fixed: signature verification now requires host header in signed headers list
